reasoning: The market asks if the NASA GISTEMP global temperature anomaly for October 2025 will be less than 1.10 ºC above the 1951-1980 baseline. Evidence indicates that global temperatures have been dropping from the highs of the 2023/2024 El Niño as the ENSO cycle moves toward neutral/La Niña conditions. In May 2025, the GISTEMP anomaly was reported as 1.07 ºC. Additionally, lower-troposphere anomalies in August 2025 (UAH +0.39 ºC vs 1991-2020) show significant cooling compared to the previous year. As the cooling effects of the developing La Niña typically intensify into the Northern Hemisphere autumn, there is a strong likelihood that the October 2025 anomaly will remain below the 1.10 ºC threshold. Given the inherent volatility in monthly anomalies, I estimate a 75% probability that the October GISTEMP figure will be under 1.10 ºC.
